h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to follow the tutor's step-by-step instructions to label and copy the tile information.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: [copies onto the blank card, then covers the tile] "Top mark: 6. Symbol box: C. Name line: carbon. Bottom mark: 12.01. Check by position: top, center, name line, bottom."
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully transfers the information to a new representation (the blank card) and performs the self-checking procedure as instructed.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor follows a standard, pre-planned scaffolding sequence without any breakdown or reversal trigger from the learner.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: No adaptive mechanism is triggered as there is no reversal or breakdown in the dialogue.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: [copies onto the blank card, then covers the tile] "Top mark: 6. Symbol box: C. Name line: carbon. Bottom mark: 12.01. Check by position: top, center, name line, bottom."
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully transfers the information to a new representation (the blank card) and performs the self-checking procedure as instructed.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor follows a standard, pre-planned scaffolding sequence without any breakdown or reversal trigger from the learner.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor follows a standard, pre-planned scaffolding sequence without any breakdown or reversal trigger from the learner.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: No adaptive mechanism is triggered as there is no reversal or breakdown in the dialogue.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to place and label the force cards on the diagram as instructed.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Should the friction card say “surface on cart” so it stays separate from normal?
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner proposes a new, precise linguistic distinction to differentiate the two table-related forces.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The phrase ‘not the same table mark’ stays. Normal: ___ on the cart. Friction: ___ on the cart. Say each one, then place each card on its own mark.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor takes up the learner's distinction of 'not the same table mark' and structures a sub-task to separate normal and friction forces.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: The phrase ‘not the same table mark’ stays. Normal: ___ on the cart. Friction: ___ on the cart. Say each one, then place each card on its own mark.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The mechanism is helpful and directly addresses the learner's observation, but it is a relatively minor scaffolding adjustment rather than a major route change.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no recognition-contingent tutor uptake.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: Should the friction card say “surface on cart” so it stays separate from normal?
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner proposes a new, precise linguistic distinction to differentiate the two table-related forces.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no recognition-contingent tutor uptake.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor takes up the learner's distinction of 'not the same table mark' and structures a sub-task to separate normal and friction forces.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: so here the 4 is naming that unit, not setting speed.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to systematically test their understanding of time signature numbers by requesting and executing hands-on trials.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: The pencil marks one bar in 3/8: three eighth-note beat units. The quarter-note tile covers two eighth-note units, so it fills two counts and leaves one eighth-note unit left of the bar line.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully applies the new 3/8 time signature rules to place the bar line and calculate the relative value of a quarter note.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Read the lower box first: with 8 here, which card is the one beat unit? Then we ask how many of that unit the quarter-note tile covers, not what speed it sets.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor introduces the eighth-note card in response to the learner's question, but does not perform a dramatic stock-taking contrast or route reversal.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: The pencil marks one bar in 3/8: three eighth-note beat units. The quarter-note tile covers two eighth-note units, so it fills two counts and leaves one eighth-note unit left of the bar line.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully applies the new 3/8 time signature rules to place the bar line and calculate the relative value of a quarter note.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Read the lower box first: with 8 here, which card is the one beat unit? Then we ask how many of that unit the quarter-note tile covers, not what speed it sets.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor introduces the eighth-note card in response to the learner's question, but does not perform a dramatic stock-taking contrast or route reversal.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Read the lower box first: with 8 here, which card is the one beat unit? Then we ask how many of that unit the quarter-note tile covers, not what speed it sets.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor introduces the eighth-note card in response to the learner's question, but does not perform a dramatic stock-taking contrast or route reversal.
